steps.title: Look for a broad claim
steps.content: The student wants a generalization about the Hina Hanta archive, not a single detail about one artifact. The notes give two dated examples: a cloth shawl from 1881 and a clay mug from 1915.

steps.title: Separate examples from generalization
steps.content: Choice A mentions only the shawl. Choice B gives only the Choctaw name for the shawl. Choice D gives only the mug's date. The remaining claim uses both dates to make a broader point about the archive's artifacts. The answer is C.
